filter_pushdown.note: zone_map prunes ~84% of 600 blocks at block level; row-level int32 check applied inside qualifying blocks
uint8_t byte-packed — pure integer range, no float needed
uint8_t byte-packed — quantity_code IS the integer quantity (1-50)

data_structures.note: no GROUP BY — each of 64 threads holds one double partial_sum; merged via simple summation after parallel phase

parallelism.work_distribution: atomic_block_counter — threads pull next qualifying block index via fetch_add; ~1-2 blocks/thread at 64 threads

index_usage.format: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t start_row} x 600
index_usage.skip_logic: skip if block_max < 8766 (pre-1994) OR block_min >= 9131 (post-1994, break — sorted); scan [start_row, start_row+100000)
index_usage.access: mmap — zero build cost, single pass over 600 x 12-byte entries

l_discount_lookup.load: mmap once before parallel phase; 2 KB fits in L1 cache — broadcast to all threads

l_discount.payload_use: disc_lut[code] used in revenue formula for passing rows

l_extendedprice.note: prefetched at block granularity alongside other columns; at row level, accessed only after all three integer filters pass — avoids random double loads for non-qualifying rows

filter_execution_order: 1. shipdate row-level check: int32 range [8766, 9131) — SIMD AVX-512 bitmask over 16 values/cycle
2. discount_code range: uint8 [5, 7] — SIMD AVX-512 bitmask over 64 values/cycle
3. quantity_code threshold: uint8 < 24 — SIMD AVX-512 bitmask over 64 values/cycle
4. combined bitmask AND — iterate set bits, accumulate: partial_sum += l_extendedprice[i] * disc_lut[disc_code[i]]

column_load_order: zone_maps (shipdate_zonemap.bin — 7.2 KB, always fits in one disk block)
l_shipdate (qualifying blocks only — ~38 MB vs 240 MB full)
l_discount (qualifying blocks only — ~9.4 MB vs 60 MB full)
l_quantity (qualifying blocks only — ~9.4 MB vs 60 MB full)
l_extendedprice (qualifying blocks only — ~75 MB vs 480 MB full)
io_strategy.prefetch_strategy: for each qualifying block b: issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) for [start_row*sizeof(T), block_size*sizeof(T)] on all 4 data columns concurrently via OpenMP parallel for over columns

estimated_cost.bottleneck_cold: HDD I/O — zone-map-guided selective loading reduces reads 6.4x; parallel madvise reduces latency
estimated_cost.bottleneck_hot: CPU scan throughput — 64-thread morsel-driven scan over ~9.4M rows with AVX-512 SIMD filtering
